question.text: According to the IFSCA Guidelines, which sanctions lists must a Regulated Entity screen against?

answer.text: The ISIL (Da'esh) & Al-Qaida Sanctions List and the 1988 Sanctions List, along with other UNSC Resolutions circulated by IFSCA
Only domestic lists published by the Ministry of Home Affairs
Only the EU sanctions list as IFSC deals with international clients

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Section 51A of UAPA, Regulated Entities must screen against: (a) The ISIL (Da'esh) & Al-Qaida Sanctions List (individuals and entities associated with Al-Qaida); and (b) The 1988 Sanctions List (individuals and entities associated with the Taliban). Plus any other UNSC Resolutions circulated by IFSCA.
**The Trap:** The screening is against UN sanctions lists (not OFAC or EU lists specifically), implemented through India's UAPA framework. Details of matching accounts must be reported to FIU-IND and the Ministry of Home Affairs.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Sanctions screening appears in both IFSCA-01 and Series XXIV. IFSCA-01 has a stronger emphasis because of the international clientele — the probability of sanctions hits is higher in an IFSC context.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Two key UN lists: ISIL/Al-Qaida list and the 1988 (Taliban) list. These are periodically updated. IFSCA may circulate additional UNSC resolutions for compliance.
